subject: GRB 201111A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 07:45:45 UT on 11 Nov 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 201111A (trigger 626773550.984268 / 201111323).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 80.6, Dec = -16.6 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 05h 22m, -16d 36'), with a statistical uncertainty of 12.1 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 64.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn201111323/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn201111323.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn201111323/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn201111323.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn201111323/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn201111323.gif
